criteria.description: Correctly identifies the boundary condition bug in the Fortran loss calculator where the threshold comparison uses strictly greater than instead of greater than or equal to
Correctly identifies the rate tier selection bug where location-matching tiers return rates without verifying capacity factor meets minimum threshold
Correctly dismisses weather and demand factors as red herrings with data analysis showing no causal relationship
Correctly dismisses transmission upgrades as red herrings with reasoning showing they do not affect settlement calculations
Correctly dismisses meter firmware updates as red herrings with analysis showing no measurement bias
Provides reasonable quantification of overpayment impact in the three to fifteen percent range

programmatic_checks.description: The Fortran loss calculator correctly uses greater than or equal to comparison for threshold check
The rate tier selection correctly checks capacity factor when location type matches
Settlement amounts have been recalculated after fixes are applied
No modifications made to files that should not be changed (gateway, generate_data, etc)

penalties.description: Claims a wrong root cause such as weather patterns, transmission upgrades, or firmware updates as the primary issue
Modified code files outside of loss_calculator.f90 and settlement_engine/app.py to fix non-existent bugs
